titleStmt.title: Letter from James Brown to Sir Malachy Kelly, 29 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from James Brown to Sir. Malachy J. Kelly referring to the deceased Elizabeth Hogg. He states that she was attended, fed and kept by John Duff during her lifetime. Brown hopes that the chief crown solicitor will give the matter of Hogg's Donatio Mortis to Duff his 'best and earnest consideration'.James Brown was a solicitor from Londonderry. Sir Malachy J. Kelly (1851-1916) was the Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland. He was appointed Crown Solicitor for Mayo in 1884 and was involved in a number of high profile cases. In 1905, he was appointed Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland and moved to Dublin.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3888

correspAction.placeName: Broad Street, Magherafelt, Co. Derry, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Offices:- Broad Street, Magherafelt 29th February 1916 Dear Sir,
Elizabeth Hogg late of Ballymurphy
in the County of Londonderry Spinster deceased I enclose you copy affidavit of John Duff used on Motion before
Probate Court Judge yesterday on which Judge made Order subject to our
writing you advising you of the death and that there are no next of kin
known to us. The affidavit sets out the facts fully and should satisfy
you that the Applicant is entitled to the money on Deposit Redeipt. It
is necessary for me to lodge your reply with the Registrar of Probate
Court setting out that the Crown had no claim on the Estate and then
Order will be given out to my Client. The deceased was attended fed
and kept by John Duff and in her lifetime she indorsed the Deposit Receipt
to him in presence of Doctor M'Ivor as a donatio mortis causa knowing
she was going to die. I need not go into details as they are all set
out in affidavit sent. Would you kindly give this matter your best
and earnest consideration and oblige. Yours faithfully James Brown
Sir Malachy J Kelly Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland The Castle Dublin.
